channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Sonic Producer Says Streets of Rage Film Was Always His Goal
item.content:encoded:
Dmitri Johnson, the producer of the Sonic the Hedgehog movies, is attached to numerous video game adaptations, and the one he’s most excited about is the Streets of Rage project. He’s made no bones about it: the opportunity to make a Streets of Rage film is what excited him about attaching himself to the Sonic movies.

While the 4th Sonic the Hedgehog film is well into production and will feature Amy Rose’s story, the Streets of Rage film is only in the scripting phase. According to a Sega financial filing, the project will be animated, and Johnson is eager to lend his expertise.

Streets of Rage Movie in Works, Producer Hints at Talent

Speaking to Stephen Totilo of Game File at the inaugural iicon event for video game executives, Johnson joked that working on the Sonic franchise was the gateway drug into making the movie he actually wanted.

“When people ask us now, like, ‘How do you find that you go after for any projects?,’ We selfishly say, ‘We’re trying to make stuff for ourselves,’” he said.

He almost spoiled more news, but promised more would come soon.

“I almost broke news,” Johnson added. “There will be news. It’ll involve some ‘exciting talent around that one.’”

In addition to the Sega projects he’s attached to, he’s also attached to many more in the world of gaming. These projects include the live-action Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 film and a Slime Rancher film. Check out his full IMDB here.

Learn more about the upcoming Sega projects revitalizing classic properties through the Sega Universe branding at this link. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Escape from Tarkov Devs Confirm Experimental Spawn Changes on Lighthouse
item.content:encoded:
Escape from Tarkov’s more eagle-eyed players started noticing a subtle change this week. They saw that, in certain raids on Lighthouse, they were spawning to the north of the map, above the water treatment plant, a place where PMCs never previously surfaced.

The developers at Battlestate Games then published a blog post confirming these changes, stressing that they’re experimental and will evolve based on incoming feedback. Fans have already voiced their biggest concerns about the map, most of which go far beyond spawn locations.

Lighthouse Spawn Changes Arrive in Tarkov

In a new blog post, Battlestate Games explained a few things about Lighthouse, one of the largest and most complex maps in Escape from Tarkov. It’s considered especially complicated because of the presence of Rogues, heavily armed guards with mounted weapons and an incredible line of sight over much of the map.

Based on feedback and suggestions from the community, the team confirmed that spawn points along the shore will not be removed, as they’re ‘currently necessary’ on the map. They also confirmed that the map’s inherent layout will not be changed, as this would be too much work, and the team is focused on optimization and upcoming events.

In recent years, almost every major map in Escape from Tarkov has undergone an overhaul, adding new areas and expanding what might have been the same for a very long time.

What has changed is the location of certain PMC spawns on Lighthouse. It was explained that PMCs can now spawn in the north of the map, near the train yard and above the water treatment plant. Elsewhere, PMCs’ spawn points have been adjusted as needed to keep the southern part of the map less congested.

BSG then requested opinions, stressing that adjustments will be made based on the community sentiments regarding this change.

In the comments under the post, users pointed out that it’s not just the spawn points that are an issue on Lighthouse. The map’s base makeup, the completion fields for certain tasks, the distribution of high-tier loot areas, and the location of extraction points all came up and faced intense negativity.

Do you think the changes to Lighthouse are that deep? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Black Flag Resynced has hit one million wishlists

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: The Last of Us Fans Kickstart Effort to Revive Online Game
item.content:encoded:
The Last of Us Online was canceled way back in 2023, but that decision has long rankled within the post-apocalyptic gaming community. It would have been a third-person multiplayer shooter, and from some internal accounts, it would have been a stellar title.

Unfortunately, it was never meant to be. Even today, fans are still yearning for The Last of Us Online, so much so that hundreds of users have signed a petition dedicated to bringing the project back to life.

The Last of Us Online Petition Gains Traction

On April 30, a fan of The Last of Us named Kristopher McCabe opened a petition on Change.org, requesting that Naughty Dog bring back to life the canceled multiplayer game that was shut down in 2023.

Within 12 hours, almost 400 verified signatures were secured, and a few page refreshes on the petition site showed the cadence of them coming in.

In a post on social media, McCabe, publishing under the name Brokowski, said:

It’s time to show PlayStation we will support this game. Please sign the petition so we can get some traction.

Just two days ago, the former director of the now-canceled shooter explained that the game would have been ‘amazing’ and that developers regarded it as ‘the best multiplayer game they’ve ever played.’

It was also revealed last month that The Last of Us Online was canceled 24 hours before the public was made aware of the decision, putting developers in a shocking position.

At the tail end of March, Neil Druckmann, the creator of the series, suggested that there are still a few stops on the road ahead, remaining cryptic and making nothing clear.

While the petition will likely amount to nothing, given that Naughty Dog is hard at work on Intergalactic and a ‘secret game’, it’s nice to know that fans still care enough to try.

Have you signed The Last of Us Online’s petition? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Warhorse Studios has responded to AI criticisms

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Warhorse Faces AI Criticism with Repeated Statements of Hiring ‘Actual Humans’
item.content:encoded:
Warhorse Studios, the developer behind the award-winning Kingdom Come series, recently hosted a deep-dive AMA on Reddit. The event landed mere weeks after the company found itself in hot water after firing a translator and replacing them with an AI solution.

The Q&A event drew intense criticism from the community, with many questions surfacing about this situation and what Warhorse is doing to rectify it. While their comments were kept confidential for HR reasons, they repeatedly stressed that they’re in the midst of hiring actual humans to do the same job.

Warhorse Addresses AI Firing Scandal

In March, a former Warhorse Studios employee claimed they were dismissed from the company and replaced with AI. The individual, Max, explained that they were working on translations for Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2, before their role was ‘abruptly terminated.’

The former employee wrote on social media:

I was invited to a meeting and promptly told that, in an effort to ‘make the company more effective’ and ‘save finances’, as of next month, my position at the company would become ‘ obsolete’ in favour of using AI for all translations going forward.

In the recent Reddit AMA, this topic became a point of contention for many of those firing questions at Warhorse. Most of the questions concerning the dismissal were met with boilerplate, HR-cleared comments:

This touches on internal HR matters, so I can’t comment on any present or past employee. That would be deeply unprofessional.

But those answering the questions, which included some of the higher-ranking team members at Warhorse Studios, mentioned numerous times that they’re hiring human translators:

What I can say is that we are currently in the process of hiring new English translators. Actual humans.

And in another comment:

Definitely. We are currently in the process of hiring new translators. Yes, actual humans. Plural.

If you are a native English speaker with a deep knowledge of Czech language and culture, send me a message.

When one user joked that they would use AI to write their questions, the team insisted it’s better to craft their own because it feels more genuine and more human.

The team tripled down (or quadrupled down) when talking about the studio’s next game:

We plan to have at least the same number of human translators on the next project as we had on KCD2. We are currently hiring English translators. Actual humans.

In February, Kingdom Come’s writer and director, Daniel Vávra, sent the community into a downward spiral when he said that AI is ‘here and it’s not going anywhere.’ His role has since changed at the company, and he’s trying to push the series into alternate media.

Do you think the Warhorse AI situation is that big an issue? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Warhorse has addressed what’s coming next for Kingdom Come

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Warhorse Addresses What’s Next for Kingdom Come: ‘We’re Already Moving Forward’
item.content:encoded:
Last year, Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 dropped to a whirlwind reception, shattering expectations, pulling in millions of players, and going on to be nominated for awards, including at the recent BAFTA Games Awards.

However, now that the game’s final piece of DLC has long since been delivered, fans are wondering what comes next for the franchise, which has been around for almost a decade. During a recent Reddit AMA, the folks at Warhorse Studios, the game’s developer, explained what’s happening.

Hard at Work

The Reddit AMA featuring some prolific folks from Warhorse Studios faced some teething issues, specifically around the recent laying off of a translator, who was later replaced with an AI-driven solution. In the wake of the PR nightmare, the team sought to patch things up with a transparent Q&A, during which they specifically addressed that situation, and many others.

As you can imagine, many questions were levelled at the team concerning Kingdom Come: Deliverance 3, a game that might never come to pass. For a while, it was rumored that Warhorse had pivoted to focus on a Lord of the Rings RPG, but this was later debunked.

In one question, the team was asked if anything else was being delivered for Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2, which had just short of a year of content before being effectively sunsetted. In response, Viktor Höschl, the Head of Art at Warhorse, explained:

No more content planned for KCD2, aside from possible patches. We’re already moving forward.

But to what?

Many questions were met with the same answer from the multiple Warhorse team members overseeing the AMA:

We are of course, hard at work on our next game. But… Like you guessed we can’t tell you much about it, not yet. What I can say though, we’ll be sticking with what we know, RPGs. Don’t expect us to go rogue and make an NBA game!

And in a similar vein, Ondřej Bittner, Content Director, said:

We are hard at work on … something. Obviously we cannot disclose any details but I can share that it is a huge immersive RPG 🙂

That doesn’t confirm or deny if another Kingdom Come game will ever surface, but it seems unlikely. In recent months, the game’s lead actor, Tom McKay, has busied himself soaking up other roles in film and television, while Luke Dale, who plays Hans Capon, has taken up a successful streaming venture, building up his content portfolio online and gaining a mighty following.

Do you think we’ll ever get another Kingdom Come: Deliverance game? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Atomfall is becoming a television show

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Windrose Surpasses 1.5 Million Sales
item.content:encoded:
Windrose has surpassed another sales milestone.

Kraken Express and PocketPair released Windrose, a pirate-themed PvE MMO survival game, on April 14, and in just 2 weeks, the game has sold 1.5 million copies. Kraken Express announced the milestone in a Steam post. Along with the announcement of selling over 1.5 million copies, the development team released the game’s accolades trailer.

“As Windrose is sailing into the third week, we’d like to present you with this accolades / thank you trailer. That’s a token of our appreciation for your support, patience, and passion,” the message reads. “Hopefully, you will like it and chuckle at some of the funniest Steam reviews we’ve put in there. With this trailer, we also celebrate another milestone – over 1,500,000 copies sold. The community keeps growing, the feedback keeps flowing, and looking at this, we feel huge gratitude. Thank you for this opportunity, captains!”

What’s Next for Windrose?

Recently, the team at Kraken Express announced that they would soon shift focus to the game’s first major update. Plans to introduce a load of new content, including a new biome, the team has said it will take at least 6 months to publish the update. While that might sound like a long wait, they want to ensure the quality of the update and the content provided.

“This might sound like a long time, but we believe it will allow us to roll out an exciting and meaningful expansion to the game of appropriate quality. In other words, we will work very hard so it’s worth the wait,” the team said.

Also, more information about the game’s roadmap is slated for release in the summer. Learn more here.

What do you think of Windrose so far? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1055)—Answers for Friday, May 1
item.content:encoded:
It’s a whole new month! Do you agree that 2026 is unravelling far too quickly? We’re already welcoming May, and it feels like just weeks ago that we were ushering in the new year. Let’s start this month the right way, by keeping up that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll pick up the best NYT Connections hints and solutions you can find anywhere online.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (May 1)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • POUR
  • WAX
  • ALE
  • HIVE
  • CREST
  • HONEY
  • MIX
  • COMB
  • BUFF
  • WIGHT
  • WATTLE
  • CITRINE
  • SHINE
  • BEAK
  • AMBER
  • POLISH

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Things you’d do to make something glossy
  • Green – Different terms for hues of gold
  • Blue – Things you’d find on a bird’s head
  • Purple – Numbers with a single letter changed

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, May 1:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, May 1 (#1055)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – BUFF, POLISH, SHINE, WAX
  • Green – ALE, AMBER, CITRINE, HONEY
  • Blue – BEAK, COMB, CREST, WATTLE
  • Purple – HIVE, MIX, POUR, WIGHT

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-may-1-1055/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Michael B. Jordan’s Battlefield Movie Ignites Reported Bidding War
item.content:encoded:
A Battlefield bidding war has ensued as the video game adaptation has five top-tier studios vying for the rights to the film.

Electronic Arts‘ lucrative first-person shooter video game franchise is being adapted into a new film, with Michael B. Jordan attached. Jordan, who recently won an Oscar for his role in Sinners, is slated to produce and potentially star in the film. Now, a new report shows just how much interest there is in acquiring the film’s rights.

Battlefield Movie Sparks ‘Bidding War of the Year’

According to TheWrap, Warner Bros. Discovery, Amazon MGM Studios, Sony, Universal, and Netflix have all submitted bids for the rights to adapt the military shooter into a video game. The report even dubs the negotiations as “shaping up to be the biggest bidding war of the year.”

In addition to the aforementioned Michael B. Jordan, Christopher McQuarrie is also attached to the project. McQuarrie is slated to write, direct, and produce the project. He’s known for writing films such as Top Gun: Maverick and several Mission: Impossible movies. He’s also listed as a writer on the 1995 film The Usual Suspects.

Battlefield Is Far From the Only Forthcoming Video Game Adaptation

In 2026, it seems video game properties are primarily focused on expanding into other media. God of War, Atomfall, Assassin’s Creed, and Fallout are all major video game franchises that have received TV adaptations. Learn more about the recently announced Atomfall adaptation here.

Meanwhile, The Legend of Zelda, Super Mario, Sonic the Hedgehog, Resident Evil, and more are all set to appear in theaters. The teaser trailer for the upcoming Resident Evil film just dropped and can be seen here.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor everything regarding the upcoming Battlefield film and report any updates.

What are you hoping to see from a Battlefield movie? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News
